page: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most standard workloads. The server achieves middle-tier scores in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing. However, raw compute benchmarks show some limitations, with stress-ng single-core performance falling into the bottom 10% and multi-core performance in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also weak, though cached small-block read bandwidth remains average. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as AES encryption and gzip compression, perform at an average level.

Given its compute-focused 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the c5a.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated compute resources without massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for tasks like static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, where its average Redis SET and database performance are sufficient. It is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance drops significantly.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 3.3 GHz and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and lacks local storage. Benchmark data shows average performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, software compilation, and static web serving, though raw stress-ng CPU metrics and uncached memory bandwidth are weaker. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, it offers a balanced, cost-effective profile for compute-focused workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, while being less optimal for memory-heavy tasks or large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered compute instance with a balanced memory-to-core ratio for standard web serving and software compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation
Delivers average performance in software compilation, web serving, and image processing
Best suited for moderate database operations, static web serving, and CI/CD pipelines
